title: Add hook leader-elector, checksum-based idempotency and migration dry-run values for private installer
preview: Motivation: on air-gapped and multi-operator installs we observed multiple init/postupgrade hooks racing which caused duplicate migrations and transient secret reconciliation failures. This PR introduces a lightweight leader-elector pattern for Helm hook jobs, a checksum-based idempotency guard to short-circuit unch...
text: Motivation: on air-gapped and multi-operator installs we observed multiple init/postupgrade hooks racing which caused duplicate migrations and transient secret reconciliation failures. This PR introduces a lightweight leader-elector pattern for Helm hook jobs, a checksum-based idempotency guard to short-circuit unchanged runs, and a configurable migration dry-run mode intended for safer private upgrades. Summary of changes: 1) templates/_hook_helpers.tpl - new leaderElector macro and checksum helpers; 2) templates/checksum-configmap.yaml - create a stable configmap with a computed checksum footprint for user-supplied migration/config; 3) templates/init-job.yaml and templates/migration-job.yaml - use leader election annotation and skip when checksum matches; 4) values.schema.json and values.yaml - add migrate.dryRun, hooks.leaderElector.enabled, hooks.leaderElector.lockTTL, hooks.check...

title: helm: introduce cost-profiles chart with QuantConfig CRD and exporter
preview: Motivation: Provide a Helm-native way to ship operator-friendly defaults for batching, cache presets, and quantization profiles so platform deployers can opt into cost-optimization automation during chart install/upgrade. This PR adds a new 'cost-profiles' Helm chart that bundles a QuantConfig CRD, a lightweight exp...
text: Motivation: Provide a Helm-native way to ship operator-friendly defaults for batching, cache presets, and quantization profiles so platform deployers can opt into cost-optimization automation during chart install/upgrade. This PR adds a new 'cost-profiles' Helm chart that bundles a QuantConfig CRD, a lightweight exporter sidecar that emits curated presets to the control-plane API, and opinionated values for batching/caching defaults. Key features: 1) QuantConfig CRD: a cluster-scoped CRD model that encodes quantization tiers (fp16, int8, q4_0), acceptable accuracy degradation thresholds, and rollout tags; 2) exporter: a small Deployment (exporter sidecar) that reads installed CR instances and posts signed preset bundles to the Redwood control plane or dumps them to a configured S3 bucket; 3) values.yaml presets: safe-by-default batch sizes, KV cache TTLs, and host-level annotations to...

title: introduce-postupgrade-cleanup-hook-and-explicit-values-schema-for-private
preview: Motivation: Private installs occasionally leave transient cluster objects (temp PVs, breadcrumb ConfigMaps) after upgrade paths that changed storage/retention policies. Operators need a safe, opt-in post-upgrade cleanup hook that can be enabled in values and validated by an explicit values.schema.json. This PR imple...
text:
Motivation: Private installs occasionally leave transient cluster objects (temp PVs, breadcrumb ConfigMaps) after upgrade paths that changed storage/retention policies. Operators need a safe, opt-in post-upgrade cleanup hook that can be enabled in values and validated by an explicit values.schema.json. This PR implements an optional post-upgrade cleanup Helm hook, adds a JSON schema for values validation, and refactors common helpers so init and post-upgrade hooks share templating primitives.

What I changed (summary): commits: 1) add values.schema.json and update values.yaml defaults; 2) introduce templates/hooks/post-upgrade-cleanup.yaml implementing a Job hook guarded by .Values.postUpgrade.cleanup.enabled; 3) refactor templates/_helpers.tpl to expose hook labels and common container/env helpers shared with init-job.yaml; 4) add a minimal integration test charts/private/tests/upgra...

title: decouple-init-configmap-and-introduce-tenant-rotation-operator-helpers
preview: Motivation: In private installs we need a small, immutable init ConfigMap consumed by several init jobs and a lightweight tenant-rotation helper that can be turned on/off independently. This PR splits the monolithic init template into a minimal configmap, introduces a tenant-rotation helper template and CRD scaffold...
text: Motivation: In private installs we need a small, immutable init ConfigMap consumed by several init jobs and a lightweight tenant-rotation helper that can be turned on/off independently. This PR splits the monolithic init template into a minimal configmap, introduces a tenant-rotation helper template and CRD scaffold for use by on-prem operators, and tightens the values schema for preflight probes and secret mapping. Changes include a compatibility shim so existing installs are not broken; rollout plan below. Checklist: - split chart/templates/init.yaml into configmap + init-job templates; - add charts/_helpers.tpl functions for secretName and rotationLabel; - add values.schema.json entries for preflight.probes and rotation.enabled; - add templates/tenant-rotation-crd.yaml (scaffold) and templates/tenant-rotation-job.yaml (opt-in); - update NOTES.txt and README with operator opt-in ste...

title: namespace-scoped init phases and per-tenant template overlay support for private installs
preview: Motivation: Private installs need safer, tenant-scoped initialization sequences and a maintainable way to apply per-namespace template overrides without adding global init jobs or replicating chart variants. This PR introduces two orthogonal capabilities: 1) namespace-scoped init phases that run initPods/Jobs limite...
text:
Motivation: Private installs need safer, tenant-scoped initialization sequences and a maintainable way to apply per-namespace template overrides without adding global init jobs or replicating chart variants. This PR introduces two orthogonal capabilities: 1) namespace-scoped init phases that run initPods/Jobs limited to the target namespace and sequenced using phase ordering and explicit readiness gates, and 2) a lightweight template-overlay mechanism (values.overlays) that lets operators supply per-namespace templating fragments (ConfigMap-style YAML) applied via an idempotent post-render hook.

Summary of changes: added namespace-scoped init controller helpers, augmented values schema with overlays and phase ordering, introduced a post-render overlay applier job and an associated dry-run mode, updated helper library templates to support overlay merging, and added unit tests and char...

title: conditional-init-job-and-hook-templating-to-support-idempotent-private-upgrades
preview: Problem / motivation: private deployments running older charts sometimes fail upgrades when init/rollback jobs are unguarded or when cluster topology (multi-az vs single-az) requires different init behavior. This caused a recent support case (SUP-122) where the init job re-ran on every helm upgrade and blocked subse...
text: Problem / motivation: private deployments running older charts sometimes fail upgrades when init/rollback jobs are unguarded or when cluster topology (multi-az vs single-az) requires different init behavior. This caused a recent support case (SUP-122) where the init job re-ran on every helm upgrade and blocked subsequent hooks. Goals: 1) make init/rollback jobs idempotent and conditional based on values and cluster facts, 2) provide a clear values migration path for private customers upgrading from older installers, 3) keep helm hooks robust for both air-gapped on-prem installs and VPC installs, 4) add minimal integration tests to prevent regressions. What this PR changes: - values.schema.json: introduce new top-level keys private.init.runOnce (boolean), private.init.leaderElectionConfig (string), private.upgrade.mode ("canary"|"standard"), and private.hooks.enableRollback (boolean). ...

title: helm-hook-helpers-and-values-example-generator
preview: Context: Private deployments have accumulated ad-hoc hook conditionals across multiple charts (private-installer, private-core, private-db). This causes subtle behavior divergence during upgrades/rollbacks and bloats templates. This PR centralizes hook decision logic into a chart helper and ships a small values-exam...
text: Context: Private deployments have accumulated ad-hoc hook conditionals across multiple charts (private-installer, private-core, private-db). This causes subtle behavior divergence during upgrades/rollbacks and bloats templates. This PR centralizes hook decision logic into a chart helper and ships a small values-example generator that emits curated values.yaml snippets used in docs and in chart-tests. Motivation: make hook behavior consistent (init/cron/pre/post hooks), simplify templating, and provide deterministic example payloads that can be baked into release notes and upgrade playbooks. Summary of changes: - Add templates/_helpers/hook_helpers.tpl: a single entrypoint to evaluate whether to render a hook and which annotations to attach based on values (canaryInit, rollbackPolicy, atomicFallback). - Replace duplicated conditional blocks in charts/private-installer/templates/init-jo...

title: charts: add failover-controller values + safe defaults (dry-run, approvals, rate limits)
preview:
## Summary
This PR introduces first-class Helm chart support for the regional failover automation controller. The goal is to make controlled enablement safe and consistent across staging and production, with explicit feature flags, conservative resource defaults, and guardrails that reduce risk during noisy-signal p...
text:
## Summary
This PR introduces first-class Helm chart support for the regional failover automation controller. The goal is to make controlled enablement safe and consistent across staging and production, with explicit feature flags, conservative resource defaults, and guardrails that reduce risk during noisy-signal periods.

Key points:
- Adds a `failoverController` block to the platform chart values with an explicit `enabled: false` default.
- Supports dry-run mode configuration (compute and emit audit events without executing flips) as the default behavior when enabled in staging.
- Adds optional approval hooks configuration (require on-call ack prior to execution) for production safety.
- Adds rate limiting / anti-flap knobs (max actions per hour, hold-down/settle timers) exposed as values and wired into container env/config.
- Adds recommended pod safety defaults: requests/limits, ...

title: Expose optimization config version pinning via Helm values + document rollback procedure
preview: Summary: This PR adds first-class Helm values to pin the active Optimization Config version for Redwood Private installs and documents an operator-friendly rollback flow (pin to prior version + apply). Motivation: In Hosted/Dedicated, the control plane manages versioning and rollback; for Private, operators need a d...
text: Summary: This PR adds first-class Helm values to pin the active Optimization Config version for Redwood Private installs and documents an operator-friendly rollback flow (pin to prior version + apply). Motivation: In Hosted/Dedicated, the control plane manages versioning and rollback; for Private, operators need a deterministic way to (a) hold a known-good version during upgrades and (b) roll back quickly when an optimization change causes a latency/5xx regression. This change introduces an explicit chart-level mechanism to pass the pinned version to the control plane/runtime, making the procedure consistent with the Safe Apply + Rollback project. What’s included: (1) New values: optimize.optimizationConfig.versionPin (string, optional) and optimize.optimizationConfig.allowUnpinned (bool, default true). When versionPin is set, the deployment will request that version from the Optimiza...

title: Add perf-canary Helm chart (safe defaults + region/env config)
preview: Summary: This PR adds a first-class Helm chart for the perf-canary service, with conservative defaults intended for initial prod rollout (single region, small model set) and guardrails to prevent accidental wide fanout / excess GPU overhead. The chart supports per-environment and per-region configuration via values ...
text:
Summary: This PR adds a first-class Helm chart for the perf-canary service, with conservative defaults intended for initial prod rollout (single region, small model set) and guardrails to prevent accidental wide fanout / excess GPU overhead. The chart supports per-environment and per-region configuration via values + explicit allowlists, and ships with security hardening defaults (non-root, RO filesystem, restricted SA by default) and observability integration hooks.

Context / why: perf-canary needs to be deployed consistently across regions with repeatable config (QPS caps, capacity budget, target models, label set). We also want a safe-disable/rollback path for on-call. This chart is the deployment substrate referenced by ENG-4879 and will be rolled out initially in us-east with a limited model list (ENG-4880).

Key behaviors / defaults:
- Disabled-by-default: chart does not run un...

title: Fix offline install values.schema.json validation (relax required fields + add defaults)
preview: Context: During the air-gapped pilots we hit schema validation failures on install/upgrade when using the “minimal offline” values files. The charts were technically installable, but values.schema.json was rejecting inputs due to (a) required fields that are optional in offline mode, and (b) missing defaults for fie...
text: Context: During the air-gapped pilots we hit schema validation failures on install/upgrade when using the “minimal offline” values files. The charts were technically installable, but values.schema.json was rejecting inputs due to (a) required fields that are optional in offline mode, and (b) missing defaults for fields that the templates already handle safely. This showed up most often when customers copy only the offline overrides from the docs/runbook (no hosted defaults), and when values are injected by the on-prem installer. What changed: 1) Add safe defaults for common offline paths: - global.airgapped defaults to false - image.pullPolicy defaults to IfNotPresent (offline-friendly) - imagePullSecrets defaults to [] (instead of requiring a non-empty array) - serviceAccount.create defaults to true where appropriate (templates assumed default) 2) Relax overly strict required lists: ...

title: Align chart versions and appVersion with Private offline bundle manifest metadata
preview: Problem: For Redwood Private air-gapped installs, we now ship signed offline bundles where the bundle manifest is the source of truth for versioning. During pilot runs we hit cases where Chart.yaml appVersion drifted from the bundle manifest releaseVersion, which confused support triage and (worse) created ambiguity...
text:
Problem: For Redwood Private air-gapped installs, we now ship signed offline bundles where the bundle manifest is the source of truth for versioning. During pilot runs we hit cases where Chart.yaml appVersion drifted from the bundle manifest releaseVersion, which confused support triage and (worse) created ambiguity about what exactly was verified/signed. This PR makes the bundle manifest drive Helm chart versioning/appVersion consistently and adds a CI gate so we fail closed before publishing offline chart tarballs.

Context: Offline bundle signing effort (deterministic manifest + signature verification in installer) requires all user-facing version identifiers to be consistent across: (1) bundle manifest metadata, (2) chart metadata, (3) private release tooling outputs. This PR focuses on (2) and the glue in our chart packaging jobs.

What changed: - Introduced a small version stamp...

title: Add Helm values for hardware tuning profile pack + override controls
preview: Title: Add Helm values for hardware tuning profile pack + override controls Context: Runtime now supports loading a versioned hardware tuning profile pack (H100/B200/L40S) and selecting an effective profile based on GPU SKU + deployment overrides (see runtime PRs: redwood/pr-21991, redwood/pr-22027). Dedicated/Priva...
text: Title: Add Helm values for hardware tuning profile pack + override controls Context: Runtime now supports loading a versioned hardware tuning profile pack (H100/B200/L40S) and selecting an effective profile based on GPU SKU + deployment overrides (see runtime PRs: redwood/pr-21991, redwood/pr-22027). Dedicated/Private installs need a supported way to (a) ship the profile pack bundle alongside the runtime, (b) pin the pack version, and (c) control override order without requiring ad-hoc ConfigMap edits that drift from supported configs. What this PR does: Wires profile pack configuration into the redwood-runtime chart values and templates, adds docs for operators, and keeps backward compatibility with existing runtimeConfig.* values. Key behavior is intentionally conservative: profiles remain OFF by default and the chart will not change runtime behavior unless the operator explicitly e...

title: Publish offline chart tarballs with sha256 checksums for signed Private bundles
preview: Summary: Adds a deterministic packaging step for Redwood Helm charts intended for Redwood Private offline bundles. The pipeline now produces versioned chart tarballs (.tgz) for each supported chart and emits a sha256 checksums file that can be referenced by the signed offline bundle manifest. Motivation: For air-gap...
text: Summary: Adds a deterministic packaging step for Redwood Helm charts intended for Redwood Private offline bundles. The pipeline now produces versioned chart tarballs (.tgz) for each supported chart and emits a sha256 checksums file that can be referenced by the signed offline bundle manifest. Motivation: For air-gapped installs, we need to transport a complete set of Helm charts without relying on remote chart repos. Bundle signing (separate work in the installer/release pipeline) needs stable inputs: a predictable chart archive name + a hash file that can be included in the bundle manifest and signed. This PR does NOT implement signing here; it only produces the chart artifacts and checksum outputs in a stable layout for the release tooling to ingest. Key changes: (1) New make target `make package-offline` that runs `helm dependency build` with pinned versions, packages charts to `di...

title: Add optional workload annotations/labels for offline bundle build correlation
preview: Context: For Redwood Private air-gapped installs we now ship signed offline bundles. Support needs a deterministic way to correlate a running cluster (workloads) to the exact offline bundle build that installed it, without requiring customers to upload the full bundle or logs. This PR adds optional annotations/label...
text: Context: For Redwood Private air-gapped installs we now ship signed offline bundles. Support needs a deterministic way to correlate a running cluster (workloads) to the exact offline bundle build that installed it, without requiring customers to upload the full bundle or logs. This PR adds optional annotations/labels to charts so the installer (or operators) can inject a bundle build identifier into Kubernetes objects. Summary: (1) Add a shared helper that renders bundle metadata into standard annotations/labels, (2) plumb through values in each chart with no-op defaults, and (3) document the values and ensure templates don’t change existing output unless the values are set. Details: - New values under global.offlineBundle (all optional): buildId (string), releaseId (string), manifestDigest (string), signerKeyId (string). - New standard keys used across workloads: annotations: redwood...

title: Add node validation agent DaemonSet (RBAC, resources, configurable checks)
preview:
## Summary
Adds a new Helm chart `node-validation-agent` that deploys the Provision-to-Ready node validation agent as a DaemonSet.

This chart is used by Fleet automation to run post-provision validation gates on GPU worker nodes before they are marked `Ready` in the control plane.

Key features:
- DaemonSet deploym...
text:
## Summary
Adds a new Helm chart `node-validation-agent` that deploys the Provision-to-Ready node validation agent as a DaemonSet.

This chart is used by Fleet automation to run post-provision validation gates on GPU worker nodes before they are marked `Ready` in the control plane.

Key features:
- DaemonSet deployment with sane defaults for GPU nodes (tolerations/nodeSelector/priorityClass)
- Least-privilege RBAC (read-only access to Node + Events; optional Lease for heartbeat)
- Resource requests/limits and safe pod security context
- Configurable validation checks (enable/disable + per-check timeouts) via ConfigMap
- Optional OTLP export (disabled by default) + structured logs to stdout

## Why
Provision-to-ready automation needs a consistent, repeatable way to run the same validations on every node across regions and GPU SKUs. Shipping the agent as a chart lets us:
- pin versions ...

title: Wire Private observability minimum pack (dashboards + alerts) into Helm values
preview: This PR wires the v1 "Private observability minimum pack" artifacts (Grafana dashboard bundle + Prometheus alert/recording rules) into the `redwood-private` Helm chart values, with customer-friendly toggles and sane defaults. Motivation: today Private installs often require manual copying of dashboards/rules, which ...
text: This PR wires the v1 "Private observability minimum pack" artifacts (Grafana dashboard bundle + Prometheus alert/recording rules) into the `redwood-private` Helm chart values, with customer-friendly toggles and sane defaults. Motivation: today Private installs often require manual copying of dashboards/rules, which makes upgrades brittle (e.g., empty dashboards after upgrade) and makes it harder for Support/SRE to assume a baseline. This change makes the minimum pack installable via `values.yaml` and version-pins the artifact bundle so air-gapped installs can package it reliably. What’s included: (1) Dashboards: ships a versioned dashboard bundle as ConfigMaps that work with the Grafana sidecar pattern. Customers can enable/disable per-category (runtime, control-plane, infra) and choose the label/key used by their Grafana sidecar. (2) Alerts/recording rules: ships `PrometheusRule` res...

title: Private: add deterministic pre-upgrade job ordering + timeouts (rollback-safe hooks)
preview:
## Summary
This updates Redwood Private Helm hook behavior to make in-place upgrades safer by default.

Key changes:
- Deterministic ordering for pre-upgrade jobs using `helm.sh/hook-weight` (and documentation/guardrails around required weights).
- Explicit, bounded timeouts on pre-upgrade jobs (and on key rollout w...
text:
## Summary
This updates Redwood Private Helm hook behavior to make in-place upgrades safer by default.

Key changes:
- Deterministic ordering for pre-upgrade jobs using `helm.sh/hook-weight` (and documentation/guardrails around required weights).
- Explicit, bounded timeouts on pre-upgrade jobs (and on key rollout waits) to avoid indefinite hangs during maintenance windows.
- Rollback-safe hook behaviors: consistent `hook-delete-policy` and job specs tuned to reduce stuck pods/resources.

This PR is part of the “Private upgrade + rollback mechanism” effort and is intended to reduce upgrade-related incidents caused by partially-completed hook execution or unbounded waiting. Related to ENG-4860 (ordering/readiness gates) and surfaced by SUP-18421 (DB migration step could hang and block the entire upgrade).

## Motivation / Problem
In Private upgrades we rely on Helm hooks for a small se...

title: Split Redwood Private chart into control-plane + data-plane rollout phases (with readiness gates)
preview:
## Context
Private upgrades need deterministic ordering so we never roll data-plane changes (runtime/model-cache/agent pools) until the control plane is fully upgraded and stable (API/console/authn/z, migrations, webhook controllers).

We previously tried to approximate ordering inside a single Helm release via hook...
text:
## Context
Private upgrades need deterministic ordering so we never roll data-plane changes (runtime/model-cache/agent pools) until the control plane is fully upgraded and stable (API/console/authn/z, migrations, webhook controllers).

We previously tried to approximate ordering inside a single Helm release via hook weights, but Helm still applies manifest changes as one transaction. This made it possible to partially roll DP resources even when CP was unhealthy (e.g., CP deployment stuck due to migration lock contention).

This PR introduces a clean separation:
- A **control-plane chart** installed/upgraded first
- A **data-plane chart** installed/upgraded second
- A lightweight **readiness gate** that blocks DP upgrades until CP health + migration state are OK

This matches the installer/orchestrator plan stages being implemented in redwood-private-installer (plan generation + phase...

title: enhance-helm-templates-with-sequenced-init-pipelines-and-rollback-guardrails
preview: Introduce a tenant-scoped sequenced init pipeline and safe-rollback flags to the private installer Helm charts. Motivation: existing init hooks run concurrently per release which led to sporadic race conditions on tenant resources during multi-tenant upgrades and made rollbacks risky when migrations partially applie...
text: Introduce a tenant-scoped sequenced init pipeline and safe-rollback flags to the private installer Helm charts. Motivation: existing init hooks run concurrently per release which led to sporadic race conditions on tenant resources during multi-tenant upgrades and made rollbacks risky when migrations partially applied. This PR adds a lightweight sequencing layer in templates plus a small values schema extension to control pipeline stages, concurrency, and rollback behavior. Changes include: (1) new values keys under .Values.initPipeline to define ordered stages, per-stage timeouts, and 'waitFor: <k8sCondition>' expressions; (2) templating helpers (templates/_init-pipeline.yaml, templates/_pipeline-helpers.tpl) that render Job/JobTemplate groups with an annotation-based sequencer (release-annotations used as semi-transactional locks); (3) an optional preflight stage that runs a dry-run ...

title: Add Helm values for Runtime LTS channel selection + release notes URL annotations
preview: Context: Dedicated + Private customers are asking to pin a stable Runtime LTS patch for 6–12+ months and to have a predictable way to discover “slower-change” release notes for the pinned version. This PR adds explicit Helm values to select the Runtime LTS channel/tag, surfaces the release notes URL via annotations,...
text:
Context: Dedicated + Private customers are asking to pin a stable Runtime LTS patch for 6–12+ months and to have a predictable way to discover “slower-change” release notes for the pinned version. This PR adds explicit Helm values to select the Runtime LTS channel/tag, surfaces the release notes URL via annotations, and documents expected pinning semantics for Private deployments.

What changed (high level): 1) New values to select the runtime image tag via an LTS-friendly interface (channel + version), while preserving existing explicit image.tag overrides. 2) Add standardized annotations on the runtime Deployment/StatefulSet (and optional Service) to expose the runtime version and release notes URL for operators/tools. 3) Docs: update chart README with examples and precedence rules (explicit tag override wins).

Values added: - runtime.channel: "stable" (default) | "lts" - runtime.l...

title: Add restore Job template + Helm hooks for safe restore-time validations
preview: Context: As part of Private Day-2 ops (backup/restore + DR), we need a deterministic way to run restore-time validations inside the cluster without relying on ad-hoc kubectl commands or long-lived pods. This PR adds a templated Kubernetes Job to the `redwood-private` chart and wires Helm hook annotations so validati...
text: Context: As part of Private Day-2 ops (backup/restore + DR), we need a deterministic way to run restore-time validations inside the cluster without relying on ad-hoc kubectl commands or long-lived pods. This PR adds a templated Kubernetes Job to the `redwood-private` chart and wires Helm hook annotations so validations run at the right time during `helm upgrade/install` restore workflows. The goal is to make restores safer (catch missing CRDs / wrong ordering / unhealthy control-plane) while avoiding destructive behavior (no writes, no migrations, no secret material). What’s included: (1) New `templates/restore/restore-validation-job.yaml` Job template that executes `redwood-private-installer restore validate` (or a configurable command) against in-cluster control-plane endpoints. (2) Hook annotations: `post-install,post-upgrade` with `hook-delete-policy: before-hook-creation,hook-suc...

title: private: add optional scheduled backup CronJob + service account/RBAC + resource defaults
preview: This PR adds first-class Helm support for scheduled backups in Redwood Private by introducing an optional Kubernetes CronJob and associated service account/RBAC wiring. This is the chart-side counterpart to the installer `backup create` command (ENG-9821) and the Day-2 backup/restore toolkit workstream. The CronJob ...
text:
This PR adds first-class Helm support for scheduled backups in Redwood Private by introducing an optional Kubernetes CronJob and associated service account/RBAC wiring. This is the chart-side counterpart to the installer `backup create` command (ENG-9821) and the Day-2 backup/restore toolkit workstream. The CronJob is disabled by default and can be enabled via values. It is designed to be safe-by-default (concurrency policy, history limits, backoff limits), to run in restricted clusters (no cluster-wide RBAC required), and to support both VPC and on-prem installs where outbound egress may be limited.

What’s included: (1) New values under `backup.cronJob.*` to control scheduling and runtime behavior, including schedule, suspend, startingDeadlineSeconds, concurrencyPolicy, ttlSecondsAfterFinished, and history limits. (2) `backup.serviceAccount.*` and `backup.rbac.*` knobs to create or ...

title: Vendorize Helm values and add init-backoff controller for private deploys
preview:
Motivation:
Private (VPC/on-prem) customers packaging charts for air-gapped installs need a reproducible, audited set of values that we ship and lock during a release. In addition, tenant bootstrap/init jobs in some clusters were observed to fail and retry indefinitely when external services (DB, image registry) wer...
text:
Motivation:
Private (VPC/on-prem) customers packaging charts for air-gapped installs need a reproducible, audited set of values that we ship and lock during a release. In addition, tenant bootstrap/init jobs in some clusters were observed to fail and retry indefinitely when external services (DB, image registry) were slow to respond. This causes init race conditions and makes rolling upgrades fragile.

What this PR does (single continuous description):
- Vendorizes chart values: a new charts/private/vendored-values/ directory contains per-version, normalized values.yaml fragments and a values.bundle.json with checksums. We ship a small helper script (ci/airgap/vend-values.sh) that creators can run to produce an airgap bundle. The vendored values are used by the installer pipeline when building on-prem bundles so customers get a deterministic, reviewed values set instead of relying on ...
